summary: Anchor-lang sighash variants tested against `sources/kamino/klend/tests/fixtures/klend.so` (deployed-BPF binary). All candidate discriminators fail to match the program's runtime hashed-first-bytes check. The klend BPF rejects every standard-form discriminator with `AnchorError::InstructionFallbackNotFound (0x65 / code 101)`. This means the deployed kamino program is NOT dispatched by anchor-lang 0.29 or anchor-lang 0.31 sighash conventions. Possible explanations: (a) kamino applied a custom dispatch table via a build-time feature, (b) the deployed BPF was built with a non-standard fork of anchor-lang, (c) the BPF in tests/fixtures/klend.so was not compiled from the rust source under sources/kamino/klend/programs/klend/src/* .
binary_md5: see /sources/kamino/klend/tests/fixtures/klend.so.md5 (not computed in this session due to time)

binary_pattern_search.method: open() + find()/bytes pattern matching for 8-byte discriminator candidates
candidates_tried_including: All 60 instruction names (kebab-case and snake-case) × every prefix variant
Hash variants: bare name, global:, anchor:, anchor:ix:, sighash:
binary_pattern_search.interpretation: The klend BPF does NOT statically embed any 8-byte sighash blob for its instructions. This is consistent with anchor-lang 0.29+ compilations where sighash is computed at runtime via solana_program::hash::hash(pre_image)[..8] inside the entrypoint dispatcher. Such runtime-hash computation does NOT appear in the binary as bytes, so a static-pattern search finds nothing — which is expected, not anomalous.

verdict: Cannot drive the deployed kamino BPF from this host using direct signature-on-the-wire construction because the deployed BPF is compiled against an anchor-lang/dispatcher scheme that we cannot retroactively determine without rebuilding klend locally. We CAN run a working engine against a locally-built kamino BPF or a klend-mirror test program using a known anchor-lang version; both routes are deferred to v6.10+.
next_session_artifacts_required: Either (a) Anchor 0.29.x install complete AND klend built locally (`anchor build` against `sources/kamino/klend/programs/klend`) producing a fresh `target/deploy/klend.so`;

Or (b) a stripped-down Anchor 0.31 test program that mirrors the klend flash-loan structure (initLendingMarket + initReserve + flashBorrowReserveLiquidity + flashRepayReserveLiquidity), compiled + deployed + tested via the existing harness.
Or (c) Use an Anchor-lang 0.31 deploy of `tests/flash_loan_fuzz.ts` against an older klend binary already compatible with 0.31 sighash — would require locating such a binary.
trust_boundary: Unchanged. No findings at this stage promoted to submit_ready. Source-review honest-zero from v6.8 preserved. Discriminator probing is engineering work, not bug-discovery work.
